docs.title: Dan's Service Desk Knowledge Base
docs.text:
Searchable IT service documentation for agents and employees — procedures, policies, and reference material for day-to-day support operations.

Press :material-slash: or use the search bar — try VPN, password, or Sev 1.

docs.title: Service Desk Overview
docs.text:
Start here

Single front door for IT help — incidents, requests, and how-to guidance.

Contoso IT Service Desk is the single point of contact for technology issues and service requests. Work is tracked in tickets so nothing falls through the cracks.

docs.title: How to contact us
docs.text: Channel When to use Details Portal Preferred for most requests support.contoso.example Email Non-urgent, attach screenshots servicedesk@contoso.example Phone Outages & Sev 1 +1 (555) 010-2000 Chat Quick questions Employee portal, 08:00–17:00

docs.title: What we support
docs.text:
• Windows and managed macOS endpoints
  • Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive)
  • Corporate VPN and zero-trust access
  • Standard printers and conference room AV (hardware faults via Facilities)

docs.title: Business hours
docs.text: Monday–Friday, 08:00–18:00 local time (company holidays excluded). After-hours coverage is limited to Severity 1 incidents.

docs.title: Convert a file library to Markdown (and rebuild this whole site)
docs.text:
This guide covers two things:

  1. How to turn a whole folder (or “database”) of documents into Markdown
  2. How to build everything in this project — MkDocs Material site, structure, search, Git, and GitHub Pages

Use it when you have a pile of Word, PDF, HTML, or similar files and want a searchable docs site like this one.

docs.title: Part 1 — Convert an entire document library to Markdown
Recommended apps
docs.text:
Tool Best for Notes Pandoc Word (.docx), HTML, RTF, many formats → Markdown Industry standard CLI. Install once, script batches easily. Microsoft MarkItDown Mixed folders: DOCX, PDF, PPTX, XLSX, images, HTML Python package; strong “whole library” converter. Docling (IBM) Messy PDFs, complex layout Heavier install; excellent PDF structure recovery. LibreOffice + Pandoc Old .doc files Convert .doc → .docx first, then Pandoc.

Practical pick for most service-desk libraries

  • Word-heavy folders → Pandoc
  • Mixed Office + PDF dump → MarkItDown
  • Scanned / layout-heavy PDFs → Docling (then clean in Markdown)

There is no perfect one-click database button

Treat your shared drive, SharePoint export, or file share as the “database.” Export or copy files into one folder tree, then batch-convert into docs/.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Windows (winget)Windows (installer) 
winget install --id JohnMacFarlane.Pandoc -e
pandoc --version

Download from pandoc.org/installing.html, then open a new terminal and run pandoc --version.

docs.title: Convert one file
docs.text:
pandoc ".\source\password-reset.docx" -t gfm -o ".\docs\how-to\password-reset.md"
  • -t gfm = GitHub-Flavored Markdown (tables work well in MkDocs)
  • For older Markdown: -t markdown

docs.title: Convert an entire folder of Word files
docs.text:
Save as scripts/convert-with-pandoc.ps1 (also in this repo under scripts/):

param(
  [string]$SourceDir = ".\sample-data\word",
  [string]$OutDir = ".\docs\imported"
)

New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $OutDir | Out-Null
Get-ChildItem -Path $SourceDir -Filter *.docx -Recurse | ForEach-Object {
  $rel = $_.FullName.Substring((Resolve-Path $SourceDir).Path.Length).TrimStart('\')
  $out = Join-Path $OutDir ($rel -replace '\.docx$', '.md')
  New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path $out) | Out-Null
  Write-Host "Converting $($_.Name) -> $out"
  pandoc $_.FullName -t gfm -o $out --wrap=none
}
Write-Host "Done. Review files under $OutDir then move into docs/ categories."

Run:

cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
.\scripts\convert-with-pandoc.ps1 -SourceDir ".\sample-data\word" -OutDir ".\docs\imported"

docs.title: PDF with Pandoc
docs.text: Pandoc’s PDF read support is limited (often needs pdftotext / poppler). Prefer MarkItDown or Docling for bulk PDF.

docs.title: Option B — MarkItDown (mixed formats, whole library)
Install (in this project’s venv)
docs.text:
cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
pip install "markitdown[all]"

docs.title: Convert one file
docs.text: markitdown ".\sample-data\word\01-service-desk-overview.docx" -o ".\docs\imported\overview.md"

docs.title: Convert a whole tree
docs.text:
# Example: walk every supported file under sample-data
$src = ".\sample-data"
$out = ".\docs\imported"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $out | Out-Null

Get-ChildItem $src -Recurse -File | Where-Object {
  $_.Extension -match '\.(docx|pdf|pptx|html|htm|xlsx|csv|json|xml)$'
} | ForEach-Object {
  $name = [IO.Path]::GetFileNameWithoutExtension($_.Name) + ".md"
  $dest = Join-Path $out $name
  Write-Host "Converting $($_.FullName)"
  markitdown $_.FullName -o $dest
}

A ready script lives at scripts/convert-with-markitdown.ps1.

docs.title: After conversion — always clean the Markdown
docs.text:
Automated conversion is step one, not the finish line.

Check What to do Titles One # H1 per page; use ## / ### for sections Tables Confirm pipes align; fix broken cells Lists Prefer real Markdown lists, not leftover bullet characters Images Put under docs/assets/ and fix ![](...) paths Front matter Optional YAML at top for title/tags (this site uses it) Nav Add each new page under nav: in mkdocs.yml Filenames kebab-case.md (e.g. password-reset.md)

Do not publish raw dumps

Run mkdocs serve, click through pages, and fix anything agents would stumble on.

docs.title: Suggested folder layout after import
docs.text:
docs/
  index.md                 # home
  getting-started/
  how-to/
  policies/
  reference/
  guides/                  # meta how-tos (this page)
  imported/                # temp drop zone from converters
  assets/
  stylesheets/
sample-data/               # original Word/PDF sources (optional archive)

Move polished files out of imported/ into the right category folders.

docs.title: Part 2 — How this site was built (full recipe)
docs.text: Everything below matches what was set up for Dan's Service Desk Knowledge Base.

docs.title: 1. Project folder + Python virtual environment
docs.text:
mkdir ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
python -m pip install --upgrade pip

docs.title: 2. Install MkDocs + Material + useful plugins
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material pymdown-extensions `
  mkdocs-git-revision-date-localized-plugin `
  mkdocs-git-committers-plugin-2
pip freeze > requirements.txt
Package Why mkdocs Static site generator mkdocs-material Modern theme, search, dark mode, cards, tabs pymdown-extensions Admonitions, task lists, better fences git-revision-date-localized “Last updated” from Git history

docs.title: 3. Scaffold the site
docs.text:
mkdocs new .

Then replace the default config with a Material setup (this repo’s mkdocs.yml):

  • Theme: Material, light/dark, sticky tabs, search.suggest / highlight / share
  • Plugins: search, tags, git revision dates
  • Extensions: admonitions, tables, task lists, emoji, tabbed content
  • Custom CSS: docs/stylesheets/extra.css
  • Optional override: overrides/main.html (announcement bar)

docs.title: 4. Write (or convert) content into Markdown
docs.text:
Two paths used in this project:

  1. Author in Markdown under docs/ (fastest for new content)
  2. Keep sources in sample-data/word and sample-data/pdf, convert → clean → place under docs/

Each article should include:

  • Clear H1 title
  • Short intro
  • Numbered procedures where agents must follow steps
  • Tables for severity/SLA matrices
  • Admonitions for warnings (!!! warning, !!! danger)

docs.title: 5. Wire navigation
docs.text:
In mkdocs.yml, list every page under nav: so the header tabs and sidebar match your IA:

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Getting started:
      - Overview: getting-started/overview.md
  - How-to:
      - Password reset: how-to/password-reset.md
  - Guides:
      - Convert files to Markdown: guides/convert-files-to-markdown.md

docs.title: 6. Preview locally
docs.text:
cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000/ Press / to search. Edit a file under docs/ and the page reloads.

Build a static copy anytime:

mkdocs build
# output → site/

docs.title: 7. Git + GitHub
docs.text:
git init
# add .gitignore with .venv/ and site/
git add .
git commit -m "Initial service desk knowledge base"

Create or rename a GitHub repo, then:

git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USER/MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff.git
git branch -M master
git push -u origin master

Tools used along the way:

  • Git for Windows — CLI
  • GitHub Desktop — visual commits / publish
  • GitHub CLI (gh) — optional automation

docs.title: 8. Publish with GitHub Pages
docs.text:
In mkdocs.yml:

site_url: https://YOUR_USER.github.io/MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff/
repo_url: https://github.com/YOUR_USER/MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff

Deploy:

mkdocs gh-deploy --force

That builds the site and pushes the gh-pages branch.

GitHub → Settings → Pages → Source: Deploy from a branch → gh-pages / root.

Private repos

Free GitHub Pages requires a public repository (or a paid plan for private Pages).

Live example for this project:

https://dgooding.github.io/MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff/

CI option: .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml runs mkdocs gh-deploy on every push to master.

docs.title: 9. Visual polish (what makes it look “real”)
docs.text: Feature How Search Material search plugin + search.suggest / highlight Dark mode Theme palette toggles in mkdocs.yml Cards on home Material grid cards in docs/index.md Brand colors docs/stylesheets/extra.css Logo / favicon docs/assets/logo.svg, favicon.svg Announcement bar overrides/main.html Tags YAML tags: on pages + tags plugin

docs.title: 10. Ongoing workflow for your team
docs.text:
1. Drop new Word/PDF into sample-data/ (or a share export folder)
2. Convert with Pandoc or MarkItDown → docs/imported/
3. Clean headings, tables, links
4. Move into how-to/ / policies/ / reference/
5. Add nav entry in mkdocs.yml
6. mkdocs serve → review
7. git commit && git push  (Actions or gh-deploy publishes)

docs.title: Quick command cheat sheet
docs.text:
cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

# Preview
mkdocs serve

# Build only
mkdocs build

# Convert Word library (Pandoc)
.\scripts\convert-with-pandoc.ps1 -SourceDir ".\path\to\docx-root" -OutDir ".\docs\imported"

# Convert mixed library (MarkItDown)
pip install "markitdown[all]"
.\scripts\convert-with-markitdown.ps1 -SourceDir ".\path\to\files" -OutDir ".\docs\imported"

# Publish
git add -A
git commit -m "Add converted articles"
git push
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

docs.title: Advanced feature showcase
docs.text:
Bells and whistles mode

This page is the advanced Material for MkDocs demo linked from the four-point star icon in the header (next to search and the theme toggle). It demonstrates patterns used on high-end docs sites: Material for MkDocs reference, diagrams, tabs, annotations, cards, and more.

Open convert-to-Markdown guide Back to home

docs.title: Theme chrome (look at the header)
docs.text: Control What it does Search Full-text search with suggestions (search.suggest) and shareable results / / Palette Light, dark, or system preference This page Custom header action (theme override) Repo Jump to GitHub source Sticky tabs Top-level sections stay available while scrolling

docs.title: Admonitions (callouts)
docs.text:
Use these to make policies and SOPs scannable under pressure.

Note

Neutral context that supports the main procedure.

Summary / TL;DR

Collapse the whole article into one tight takeaway.

Info

Background or system behavior the reader should know.

Tip

Faster path or power-user shortcut.

Success

Expected healthy outcome after the steps.

Question

Common agent or end-user FAQ.

Warning

Easy to get wrong — pause before continuing.

Failure

Known bad state or failed validation.

Danger

Security, data loss, or major-incident risk.

Bug

Known defect or vendor limitation.

Example

Concrete ticket wording or sample payload.

Quote

Policy excerpt or external standard.

docs.title: Collapsible blocks
docs.text:
Expand for the long version

Extra detail lives here so the default view stays short. Perfect for edge cases, vendor IDs, and deep troubleshooting trees.

Open by default

Use ???+ when the warning must be visible immediately but can still collapse after read.

docs.title: Content tabs
docs.text:
Group OS-specific or tool-specific steps without duplicating whole pages.

WindowsmacOS / LinuxDocker (idea) 
cd ~\MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000/.

cd ~/MKDocs-figuring-out-stuff
source .venv/bin/activate
mkdocs serve

docker run --rm -it -p 8000:8000 -v ${PWD}:/docs squidfunk/mkdocs-material

Nested tabs also work for API examples:

cURLPowerShellPython 
curl -sS -X POST https://api.contoso.example/v1/tickets \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"VPN down","severity":2}'

Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post `
  -Uri "https://api.contoso.example/v1/tickets" `
  -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $TOKEN" } `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body (@{ title = "VPN down"; severity = 2 } | ConvertTo-Json)

import os, requests

r = requests.post(
    "https://api.contoso.example/v1/tickets",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['TOKEN']}"},
    json={"title": "VPN down", "severity": 2},
    timeout=30,
)
r.raise_for_status()
print(r.json())

docs.title: Code blocks with line numbers, copy, and annotations
docs.text:
Hover a code block to copy. Numbers and annotations help walk an agent through a script.

triage_ticket.py
from enum import Enum

class Severity(Enum):
    SEV1 = 1
    SEV2 = 2
    SEV3 = 3
    SEV4 = 4

def choose_severity(users_impacted: int, workaround: bool) -> Severity:
    if users_impacted >= 50:
        return Severity.SEV1  # (1)
    if users_impacted >= 5 and not workaround:
        return Severity.SEV2  # (2)
    if users_impacted >= 1:
        return Severity.SEV3  # (3)
    return Severity.SEV4
  1. Company-wide or large cohort outages start at Sev 1.
  2. Multi-user impact with no workaround is Sev 2.
  3. Single-user / small group with business still moving is Sev 3.

Inline code and keys: press Ctrl+K or / to search; config lives in mkdocs.yml.

Mark text with highlights, insertions, and strike when comparing revisions.

docs.title: Keyboard keys, abbreviations, and tooltips
docs.text:
Hover the abbreviations after they are defined:

The service desk uses the SLA for Sev 1 and may open a MIR with CSIRT when security is involved.

Tooltips on links work with Material’s content.tooltips feature:

Password reset procedure

docs.title: Buttons and icons
docs.text:
Primary action Secondary action With icon

Icon + emoji soup for scannable lists:

  • Security first response
  • Remote access
  • Messaging
  • Live agent support
  • Automation candidates

docs.title: Onboarding checklist
docs.text:
• Create identity from HR ticket
  • Assign license bundle
  • Confirm MFA enrollment
  • Close ticket after user confirmation

docs.title: Definitions
docs.text: Service request A planned ask (access, hardware, software) — not an unplanned outage. Incident Unplanned interruption or degradation of an IT service. Problem Root cause of one or more incidents; tracked separately from the break/fix ticket.

docs.title: Tables (status + data)
docs.text:
Severity Initial response Example Status Sev 1 15 minutes Email down company-wide Critical Sev 2 1 hour Regional VPN outage Major Sev 3 4 hours Single laptop print fail Normal Sev 4 1 business day Cosmetic desktop issue Low

Grid of cards for navigation-style layouts:

  • Authoring

    Standards for clear, searchable articles

    KB guidelines

    Mermaid flow, sequence, and ER charts below

docs.title: Mermaid diagrams
docs.text: Native Mermaid support (via pymdownx.superfences custom fence) — colors follow the theme.

docs.title: Service desk intake flowchart
docs.text:
graph TD
  A[User reports issue] --> B{Self-service KB hit?}
  B -->|Yes| C[Resolve without ticket]
  B -->|No| D[Open ticket in portal]
  D --> E{Security related?}
  E -->|Yes| F[Tag Security + page CSIRT]
  E -->|No| G[Tier 1 triage]
  G --> H{Known fix?}
  H -->|Yes| I[Apply fix / close]
  H -->|No| J[Escalate Tier 2]
  F --> K[Incident bridge]
  J --> L[Resolve + knowledge article?]
  I --> L
  K --> L

docs.title: Password reset sequence
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor User
  participant Portal as Self-service portal
  participant MFA as Authenticator
  participant Desk as Service Desk
  participant IdP as Identity platform

  User->>Portal: Request password reset
  Portal->>MFA: Challenge
  MFA-->>User: Approve prompt
  User->>Portal: Set new password
  alt Self-service succeeds
    Portal->>IdP: Commit password change
    IdP-->>User: Success
  else Self-service fails
    User->>Desk: Open ticket
    Desk->>User: Verify identity
    Desk->>IdP: Admin reset + force change
    IdP-->>User: Temporary password (secure channel)
  end

docs.title: Entity relationship (simplified CMDB)
docs.text:
erDiagram
  EMPLOYEE ||--o{ DEVICE : is-assigned
  EMPLOYEE ||--o{ TICKET : opens
  DEVICE ||--o{ TICKET : mentioned-in
  TICKET }o--|| SERVICE : impacts
  TICKET }o--o| CHANGE : linked-to
  SERVICE ||--o{ SLA : governed-by

docs.title: State machine — major incident
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Detected
  Detected --> Triaged
  Triaged --> Mitigating
  Mitigating --> Monitoring
  Monitoring --> Resolved
  Resolved --> Postmortem
  Postmortem --> [*]
  Triaged --> MajorBridge: Sev1 / wide Sev2
  MajorBridge --> Mitigating

docs.title: Math (when you need formulas)
docs.text:
Inline: the availability target \( A = \frac{\text{uptime}}{\text{uptime}+\text{downtime}} \).

Block:

\[ \text{SLA credit} = \max\left(0,\; R - \frac{\text{actual uptime}}{\text{month minutes}}\right) \times C \]

docs.title: Footnotes and citations
docs.text: Agents should cite the source procedure in the ticket1. For security events, attach the timeline to the CSIRT handoff2.

docs.title: Nested formatting playground
docs.text:
Blockquote for policy callouts from leadership.

Nested quote for “exact wording from the standard.”

  1. Ordered step one
  2. Nested bullet
  3. Nested bullet
  4. Ordered step two with inline code and bold / italic / both
  5. Step with a footnote marker1

docs.title: What powers this page (config map)
docs.text:
Capability Enabled via Material theme + dark mode theme.name, theme.palette Instant nav + sticky tabs navigation.* features Search suggest / highlight / share search.* features + search plugin Code copy + annotate content.code.copy, content.code.annotate Tabs, task lists, critic marks pymdownx.tabbed, tasklist, mark, tilde, caret Mermaid diagrams pymdownx.superfences → custom_fences: mermaid Icons / emoji pymdownx.emoji + Material icon sets Admonitions + details admonition, pymdownx.details Math pymdownx.arithmatex (+ MathJax if you add JS) Header star icon overrides/partials/header.html Brand CSS docs/stylesheets/extra.css

Show this to your team

This page is intentionally loud. Production SOPs can stay quieter — reuse only the patterns that improve scan-speed for agents.

  1. Knowledge articles are living documents — link the page URL, not a screenshot alone. ↩↩

docs.title: Email Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text:
KB-M365-021 · v1.6

Audience: Tier 1 agents

docs.title: Quick triage
docs.text:
Before changing settings, determine whether the issue is send, receive, calendar, or mobile-only.

  • Can the user sign in at outlook.office.com?
  • Does the issue affect one device or all devices?
  • Any recent password or MFA changes?
  • Is the mailbox full (quota warning)?

docs.title: Desktop Outlook
docs.text:
1. Repair Office apps from Settings → Apps
  2. Create a new Outlook profile if the profile is corrupt
  3. Clear Credential Manager entries for MicrosoftOffice*
  4. Check rules and Focused Inbox for missing mail

docs.title: Mobile mail
docs.text:
1. Remove and re-add the work account in Outlook mobile
  2. Confirm device compliance in Company Portal
  3. Do not recommend native iOS/Android mail for corporate accounts

docs.title: Escalate when
docs.text:
Messaging team

Mailbox corruption, litigation hold issues, transport rule failures, or hybrid mail flow problems — assign to Messaging with logs attached.

docs.title: Eligible hardware
docs.text: Item Refresh cycle Notes Laptop 3–4 years Role-based models only Monitor 5 years Max two per desk by default Headset As needed Noise-cancelling standard

docs.title: How to request
docs.text:
1. Open Service Request → Hardware
  2. Select catalog item and ship-to address
  3. Manager approval required for non-standard items
  4. Asset tag is recorded on delivery; return old kit on refresh

docs.title: Lost or stolen devices
docs.text:
Report immediately

Contact Service Desk and Security. Do not delay waiting on a replacement request.

docs.title: New Employee IT Onboarding Checklist
docs.text:
CHK-HRIT-003 · v3.0

For service desk agents and HR partners

docs.title: Before day one
docs.text:
• Create identity account from HR ticket (name, department, manager, start date)
  • Assign license bundle from role template
  • Stage laptop image (ship or pickup)
  • Add security groups (department + VPN if remote)
  • Schedule welcome email with first-login instructions

docs.title: Day one
docs.text:
• Verify MFA enrollment completed
  • Confirm email, Teams, and OneDrive sign-in
  • Map standard printers if site-based
  • Walk through password self-service registration
  • Share link to the knowledge base home

docs.title: Within first week
docs.text:
• Confirm access to role-specific applications
  • Close onboarding ticket only after user confirmation
  • Tag ticket with site and department for reporting

docs.title: Password Reset Procedure
docs.text:
SOP-AUTH-001 · v1.2

Audience: end users and Tier 1 agents

Prefer self-service. Agents use the procedure below only when self-service fails or the account is locked.

docs.title: Self-service steps
docs.text:
1. Go to password.contoso.example
  2. Enter your work email and complete the MFA challenge
  3. Choose a new password (14+ characters, mixed case, number, symbol)
  4. Sign out of all sessions, then sign back in

docs.title: Agent procedure
docs.text:
Verify identity first

Never reset a password without confirming employee ID, manager, and an approved contact method.

  1. Open the ticket and confirm identity (employee ID, manager, last four of corporate mobile or HR alternate)
  2. In the identity admin console, select Reset password and force change at next sign-in
  3. Clear temporary lockouts if present
  4. Provide the temporary password via a secure channel (never email permanent secrets)
  5. Advise MFA re-registration if prompts fail after reset
  6. Document steps and resolve the ticket

docs.title: Common issues
docs.text: Symptom What to try Password works, apps fail Clear cached credentials, restart, re-auth VPN/MFA Account locked Wait 15 minutes or clear lock in admin console MFA device lost Escalate to Identity with manager approval Shared/service account Do not reset without owner approval

docs.title: Supported devices
docs.text: Corporate networked printers, standard USB headsets, webcams, and docking stations listed in the hardware catalog.

docs.title: Printer issues checklist
docs.text:
1. Confirm the user is on the corporate network or VPN if required
  2. Reinstall the printer from the print server share
  3. Clear stuck jobs from the print queue
  4. Check toner/paper and physical error lights
  5. Escalate to Facilities for paper jams needing hardware service

docs.title: Docking and monitors
docs.text: Update dock firmware via Company Portal. Try a different USB‑C/Thunderbolt cable before replacing hardware.

docs.title: Remote Desktop Access SOP
docs.text:
SOP-EUC-011 · v1.0

Audience: support agents

docs.title: Policy
docs.text: Interactive remote control requires user consent, except break-glass admin scenarios approved by the duty manager.

docs.title: Starting a remote session
docs.text:
1. Open the remote support tool from the agent console
  2. Enter the ticket number (required for audit)
  3. Send the session invitation to the user
  4. Wait for Accept on the user device
  5. Announce actions before making system changes
  6. End the session and note the outcome in the ticket

docs.title: Prohibited actions
docs.text:
• Do not disable security tools without Security approval
  • Do not copy personal files off the device
  • Do not share your admin credentials with the user

docs.title: Software Installation Request Process
docs.text:
SOP-REQ-008 · v1.1

Only approved software may be installed on managed devices.

docs.title: Request flow
docs.text:
1. Search the software catalog for an existing package
  2. If listed → Request install (free tier tools auto-approve)
  3. If not listed → open a Service Request with:
    • Business justification
    • Cost center
    • Data classification
  4. Manager approves; Security reviews if regulated data is involved
  5. Packaging team publishes to Company Portal when approved

docs.title: SLA targets
docs.text: Request type Target Catalog install 8 business hours License add-on 2 business days New package (standard) 5 business days New package (security review) 10 business days

docs.title: VPN Access Guide
docs.text:
KB-NET-014 · v2.0

Audience: remote workers

Use the corporate VPN only when you need internal systems that are not published through the secure web gateway (legacy file shares, some finance apps, lab networks).

docs.title: Install and connect
docs.text:
1. Install Contoso Secure Access from Company Portal / Managed Software Center
  2. Sign in with your work account (SSO)
  3. Approve the MFA prompt
  4. Confirm status shows Connected and an internal IP is assigned

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Collect logs first

Use Help → Export logs before contacting the service desk.

  • Home Wi‑Fi blocking UDP — try alternate port profile or a wired connection
  • Certificate errors — restart the device; ensure date/time is automatic
  • Split tunnel — internet should still work; only corporate routes use the VPN

docs.title: Security rules
docs.text:
• Do not share VPN profiles
  • Personal devices require MDM enrollment before VPN is allowed

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Changes to production IT services are planned, reviewed, and communicated to reduce unplanned downtime.

docs.title: Change types
docs.text: Type Approval Lead time Standard Pre-approved As scheduled Normal CAB / manager 3 business days Emergency Duty manager As needed

docs.title: Service desk role
docs.text:
• Communicate maintenance windows to users
  • Monitor incidents during change implementation
  • Link related incidents to the change record

docs.title: Incident Severity Levels
docs.text:
POL-IM-002 · v1.4

Severity is based on business impact, not preference alone.

docs.title: Severity table
docs.text: Severity Definition Initial response Example Sev 1 Critical service down for many users or revenue impact 15 minutes Email outage company-wide Sev 2 Major function degraded; limited workaround 1 hour VPN down for one region Sev 3 Single user or small group; business continues 4 hours One laptop cannot print Sev 4 Low impact / cosmetic 1 business day Desktop shortcut missing

docs.title: How to choose severity
docs.text:
• Count of users affected
  • Whether a viable workaround exists
  • Regulatory, safety, or security implications
  • Production vs. test environment

docs.title: Communication
docs.text: Sev 1 and Sev 2 require status updates on the major-incident channel until resolved or mitigated.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
This SLA covers Contoso IT services delivered through the Service Desk for full-time employees and long-term contractors.

  • Incident response and resolution targets by severity
  • Service request fulfillment targets
  • Planned maintenance windows

docs.title: Availability targets
docs.text:
Core collaboration services target 99.9% monthly availability excluding announced maintenance.

Service Target availability Email / Teams 99.9% VPN / remote access 99.5% Service desk portal 99.5% Identity / SSO 99.95%

docs.title: Exclusions
docs.text: Force majeure, customer-owned networks, and unsanctioned software are excluded from SLA credits.

docs.title: When to escalate
docs.text:
Escalate when the SLA is at risk, the issue exceeds Tier 1 skills, or a major incident is declared.

Level Owner Trigger Tier 1 Service Desk Initial triage and known fixes Tier 2 Platform teams Complex config, multi-user impact Tier 3 Engineering / vendor Code defects, infrastructure faults Major Incident Duty manager Sev 1 or widespread Sev 2

docs.title: Contacts
docs.text:
• Duty manager on-call — PagerDuty schedule IT-Duty
  • Security CSIRT — security@contoso.example
  • Vendor bridge — open only after Tier 3 acknowledges

docs.title: Documentation required
docs.text: Every escalation must include timeline, impact statement, reproduction steps, and current workaround.

docs.title: Knowledge Base Authoring Guidelines
docs.text:
STD-KB-004 · v1.0

Consistent structure improves search, navigation, and handoffs between agents.

docs.title: Why structure matters
docs.text: Articles with clear titles, short procedures, and consistent headings are easier to find and faster to follow under pressure.

docs.title: Article template
docs.text:
1. Title — task-oriented (e.g. Reset your VPN client)
  2. Summary — 1–2 sentences
  3. Before you begin — prerequisites
  4. Steps — numbered, one action each
  5. Result — what success looks like
  6. Related articles — links

docs.title: Style rules
docs.text:
• Active voice, second person (you)
  • Screenshots only when the UI is non-obvious
  • Call out risk with warning or danger notes
  • Review quarterly or after major product changes

docs.title: Document structure
docs.text:
• Use clear section headings for every major topic
  • Prefer numbered steps for procedures and bullets for options
  • Group content by audience need: how-to, policy, or reference

docs.title: Importing a large file library
docs.text:
To convert an entire folder of Word/PDF files into Markdown and wire them into this site, see:

Convert files to Markdown & build this site

docs.title: Security Incident Quick Reference
docs.text:
REF-SEC-002 · Internal

Treat suspected security events as high priority.

docs.title: If you suspect a security incident
docs.text:
Examples: phishing click, ransomware symptoms, lost device with data, unexpected admin prompts, accidental external data email.

  1. Prefer preserving evidence — disconnect network if malware is active; do not wipe unless instructed
  2. Contact Service Desk and mark the ticket Security
  3. Notify Security CSIRT for confirmed or high-confidence events
  4. Do not delete emails or files that may be evidence

docs.title: Phishing reports
docs.text:
• Use the Report phishing button in Outlook
  • Forward as attachment only if the button is missing
  • Never enter credentials on linked pages from suspicious mail

docs.title: Service desk first response
docs.text: If credential theft is likely: reset password, revoke sessions, review MFA methods, then escalate to CSIRT with a timeline.
